questions.question: Which of the following empirical estimation models is a probabilistic approach?
options: B) Program Evaluation and Review Technique (PERT)
D) Program Analysis and Review Technique (PART)
questions.explanation: Bayesian Estimation is a probabilistic approach to estimation, which takes into account uncertainty and probability distributions.

questions.question: Empirical Estimation Model that uses historical data and actual project performance to estimate future project duration?
options: A) Program Evaluation and Review Technique (PERT)
questions.explanation: Three-Point Estimation is an empirical estimation model that uses historical data and actual project performance to estimate future project duration.

questions.question: Which of the following empirical estimation models uses a combination of historical data and expert judgment to estimate project duration?
options: A) Program Evaluation and Review Technique (PERT)
questions.explanation: Three-Point Estimation is an empirical estimation model that uses a combination of historical data and expert judgment to estimate project duration.

questions.question: Which of the following empirical estimation models uses a single estimate to measure project duration?
options: A) Program Evaluation and Review Technique (PERT)
questions.explanation: Three-Point Estimation is an empirical estimation model that uses a single estimate to measure project duration.

questions.question: Which of the following empirical estimation models is an iterative approach?
options: A) Program Evaluation and Review Technique (PERT)
questions.explanation: Bayesian Adaptive Reestimation is an iterative approach to estimation, which continuously updates the estimate based on changing project requirements and actual performance.
